input: Apple Inc. reported Q3 FY2024 results: revenue of $85.8B (up 5% YoY), net income $21.4B, EPS $1.40 vs $1.26 expected. iPhone revenue $39.3B (-1% YoY), Services revenue $24.2B (+14% YoY), record for Services. Gross margin 46.3% vs 44.5% prior year. Cash and equivalents $153B. Guidance for Q4: revenue $85-88B. CEO Tim Cook highlighted AI integration into iOS 18 and expanding India manufacturing. The stock rose 3.2% after-hours.
expected: BULLISH. Services segment hitting record $24.2B (+14% YoY) with expanding 46.3% gross margins signals a structural shift from hardware dependency. $153B cash reserve + AI/iOS18 catalyst and India manufacturing diversification reduce risk. Q4 guidance midpoint ($86.5B) implies continued momentum. Key risk: iPhone decline (-1%) must stabilize. Price action (+3.2% AH) confirms institutional buy-in. Outlook: accumulate on dips, 12-month target range $210-230.

input: Tesla Q2 2024: Revenue $25.2B (+2% YoY), automotive revenue $19.8B (-7% YoY), energy generation $3.0B (+100% YoY). Net income $1.48B (-45% YoY), automotive gross margin 18.5% vs 18.1% prior quarter. Deliveries 443,956 units (-5% YoY first decline since 2020). Free cash flow $1.34B. Cybertruck production ramping, 1,000 units/week target. FSD revenue recognition deferred $2.9B. Price cuts totaling 25% over 18 months. Elon Musk reaffirmed Robotaxi event October 2024 and 20-30% vehicle volume growth in 2025.
expected: CAUTIOUS/NEUTRAL. Automotive margin recovery to 18.5% is encouraging but masks structural pressure: 25% cumulative price cuts have eroded brand premium and revenue (-7% auto YoY) with no clear floor. Energy segment doubling is a real positive but too small ($3B) to offset core weakness. The $2.9B deferred FSD revenue is a non-cash accounting wildcard. Delivery decline (-5% YoY) is a red flag for growth thesis. Robotaxi and 2025 volume promises are execution-dependent and speculative. Avoid adding; watch Q3 delivery data as make-or-break signal.

input: Nvidia Q1 FY2025: Revenue $26.0B (+262% YoY, +18% QoQ), Data Center $22.6B (+427% YoY). Net income $14.9B, EPS $5.98 vs $5.59 expected. Gross margin 78.4%. Q2 guidance: $28B ±2%. Blackwell GPU architecture shipments beginning Q2, H100 still in high demand. Hyperscaler customers (Microsoft, Google, Amazon, Meta) all increasing AI capex. Supply constraints easing. $10B buyback authorized. CEO Jensen Huang noted 'accelerating demand with no demand destruction observed'.
expected: STRONG BUY. The numbers speak clearly: 78.4% gross margin at $26B scale is exceptional and expanding. Data center at $22.6B is not a one-quarter anomaly — all four hyperscalers are structurally increasing AI capex with multi-year commitments. Blackwell transition risk is real but manageable given continued H100 demand. Supply easing removes the only near-term constraint. $28B Q2 guidance (+7.7% QoQ) shows no demand cliff. The $10B buyback signals management confidence. Only risk: geopolitical (China export controls) and a black-swan hyperscaler capex pullback. Core holding; add on any >10% pullback.

input: Bed Bath & Beyond filed Chapter 11 bankruptcy January 2023. Last reported quarter: revenue $1.26B (-33% YoY), same-store sales -32%, gross margin collapsed to 16% from 38% two years prior. Inventory write-downs $100M+. $1.82B total debt vs $100M cash. Lost 4 of 5 C-suite executives in 6 months. 150 store closures announced. Vendors cutting credit terms, requiring cash-on-delivery. DIP financing $240M secured. Holiday season sales missed badly. Attempted turnaround with owned-brand strategy failed to gain traction.
expected: AVOID / HIGH BANKRUPTCY RISK. This is a textbook retail death spiral: gross margin collapse (38% → 16%) signals vendor relationship breakdown and desperate discounting simultaneously. $1.82B debt vs $100M cash with vendors demanding COD means liquidity is days/weeks dependent on DIP financing. -32% same-store sales has no floor visible. Executive exodus removes any execution optionality. The owned-brand pivot was a costly distraction during a survival crisis. DIP financing buys time, not a turnaround. Equity is likely worthless; any rally is a short opportunity. Do not hold any position.

input: Microsoft Q3 FY2024: Revenue $61.9B (+17% YoY), cloud (Azure) +21% YoY, Office 365 commercial +15%, LinkedIn +10%. Operating income $27.6B, margin 44.6%. EPS $2.94 vs $2.82 expected. AI Copilot now embedded across all product lines, 1.8M paying Copilot seats. Capital expenditure $14B (+79% YoY) for AI infrastructure. Activision contribution $900M revenue in quarter. Free cash flow $20.3B. Guidance Q4: $63.5-64.5B revenue. Azure growth expected to accelerate in H2 as AI workloads ramp.
expected: BULLISH / CORE HOLDING. 44.6% operating margin at $61.9B scale with accelerating growth is rare in large-cap tech. Azure +21% with management guiding acceleration demonstrates durable cloud share gains, not one-time AI hype. 1.8M Copilot seats is early but the monetization path ($30/user/month) represents a $650M+ ARR run rate growing rapidly. $14B capex (+79%) is aggressive but justified given Azure demand signals. FCF of $20.3B funds buybacks and dividends comfortably. Activision integration on track. Risk: capex cycle must convert to revenue — watch Azure growth for any deceleration. Strong buy on any weakness.
